headline: RM890 million in pandemic procurement still unaccounted for
context: The Auditor General's 2025 follow-up report found RM890 million in COVID-19 emergency procurement from 2020-2022 remains unaccounted for across seven ministries.

cards.big: RM890 million in COVID-19 procurement spending — still unaccounted for after 4 years
cards.sub: Emergency spending used to justify bypass of normal procurement controls

cards.big: Emergency ordinance allowed direct procurement without competitive bidding during pandemic
cards.sub: Legal exemptions meant for genuine emergency were exploited beyond their intended scope

cards.big: Unaccounted spending includes RM340 million in PPE purchases at 3-5 times market price
cards.sub: Price gouging during national crisis represents the worst form of public corruption

cards.big: Post-pandemic audit team reduced from 34 to 8 auditors in 2024 budget cuts
cards.sub: Government reduced accountability capacity for pandemic spending as public attention faded

cards.big: Reducing auditors for pandemic spending isn't moving on — it's burying the evidence
Should a dedicated pandemic procurement commission complete all outstanding audits?
